headline: Transcript: Looking at 2025
summary: Katie Martin speaks to Robert Armstrong and Aiden Reiter
body_text:
This is an audio transcript of the Unhedged podcast episode: ‘Looking at 2025’

Robert Armstrong Special note to listeners, we’re very keen to hear your questions and answer them on the air. If you have burning questions about markets, finance or the economy, send them to robert.armstrong@ft.com.

[MUSIC PLAYING]

Katie Martin It’s the most wonderful time of the year, 2025 look ahead season. This annual exercise by investment houses and big banks seems to get earlier and earlier every year.

[MUSIC PLAYING]

Still, here it is. And it’s a good way to test the market mood and try and figure out what everyone thinks will happen next. Today on the show, we’re asking, what are they asking? What are the big questions, the big puzzles to crack if you want to get 2025 right? I mean, there’s still plenty of 2024 to get wrong. (Laughter) This . . . you guys shush.

Robert Armstrong OK.

Katie Martin This is Unhedged, the markets and finance podcast from the Financial Times and Pushkin. I’m Katie
